title: Five NFL Free Agents Whose Signings May Not Pay Off Big in 2026
body:
The 2026 NFL Free Agency Period has come to a close, leaving a trail of significant signings and potentially regrettable decisions in its wake. As teams look to bolster their rosters and make a push for the playoffs, it's not uncommon for players to be overpaid or for teams to prioritize short-term gains over long-term stability. With this year's free agency period now behind us, several signings stand out as potential busts that teams may come to regret in the years to come.

The Baltimore Ravens, for example, made a significant splash by signing wide receiver Juwan Johnson to a lucrative contract extension. At first glance, the move seems like a savvy one, as Johnson has proven himself to be a reliable target for quarterback Lamar Jackson. However, with the Ravens already boasting a talented receiving corps, it's unclear whether Johnson's addition will truly make a significant impact on the team's success. Furthermore, Johnson's contract extension will come with a hefty price tag, potentially limiting the Ravens' ability to make other key additions in the future.

Another team that may be feeling buyer's remorse is the Indianapolis Colts. After a tumultuous offseason, the Colts signed wide receiver Robert Woods to a multi-year deal, in the hopes that he would provide a much-needed boost to their passing game. While Woods has certainly shown flashes of brilliance throughout his career, his age and injury history raise concerns about his ability to stay healthy and productive over the long haul. For a team that has consistently struggled to find consistency on offense, it's unclear whether Woods will be able to make a meaningful contribution.

The Chicago Bears, meanwhile, have invested heavily in their defensive line, signing pass rusher Chase Young to a lucrative contract extension. While Young has certainly shown the ability to wreak havoc on opposing offenses, his lack of production in the Bears' defensive scheme raises questions about his long-term fit with the team. Furthermore, the cost of Young's contract may prove to be prohibitive, potentially limiting the Bears' ability to make other key additions in the future.

As the NFL season heats up, it will be fascinating to see how these signings play out. Will the Ravens come to regret their decision to overpay Juwan Johnson, or will the Colts find a way to get the most out of Robert Woods? One thing is certain: the 2026 NFL season will be a wild ride, with teams and players alike vying for glory and, in some cases, struggling to justify their off-season decisions. As the season gets underway, one thing is clear: the NFL's free agency period has once again left its mark on the league, shaping the rosters and futures of teams across the league.

In the end, the true test of these signings will come not in the short-term, but in the long-term. As teams look to build sustainable success, it's not the flashy signings that will ultimately determine their fate, but rather those that prove to be wise, forward-thinking investments. With the 2026 NFL season underway, one thing is certain: the next few months will be filled with twists and turns, as teams and players navigate the ever-changing landscape of the NFL.
